demographics.living_situation: lives at home with a primary caregiver; wheelchair dependent

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: My head is pounding out of my skull, I'm sweating buckets over my chest and face, and I feel a terrible sense of panic — his caregiver here, his blood pressure on the home machine just came up as 190 over 110.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It all came on out of nowhere over the last half hour and it's getting worse — the home blood pressure reading is really high and we're frightened.

symptom_narrative.onset: about 30 minutes ago, came on suddenly
symptom_narrative.character: a pounding, throbbing headache with heavy sweating and flushing across the face and chest, and a feeling of dread/panic
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the headache is pounding out of his skull — one of the worst he has ever had
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: head, and sweating/flushing over the face and chest above the nipple line
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and escalating since it began
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps building on its own
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: sweating profusely above the chest, facial flushing, goosebumps, and a sense of panic; the caregiver mentions his catheter bag has hardly filled for about 4 hours
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the caregiver says he has had blood-pressure spikes like this before because of his spinal injury
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is very distressed and cannot settle

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a complete spinal cord injury at the mid-upper back (T4 level) from years ago — no movement or feeling below the injury
past_medical_history.how_managed: wheelchair dependent; needs help with transfers and with bowel and bladder care

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: no bladder or bowel control; uses a permanent tube in the belly (suprapubic catheter) to drain urine
past_medical_history.how_managed: indwelling suprapubic catheter, managed with caregiver support

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: known to get dangerous blood-pressure spikes because of the spinal injury

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: a tablet to put under the tongue for blood-pressure emergencies

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: wheelchair dependent, lives with a primary caregiver who is present and made the call and manages his catheter and transfers

objective_data_offered.value: 190/110 mmHg, and the caregiver says it read as high as 195/115 a few minutes later
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: home upper-arm monitor, readings taken during acute distress

objective_data_offered.value: about 52 beats per minute — slow, from the home blood-pressure machine
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: pulse figure from a home BP cuff, single reading

objective_data_offered.value: face and chest are visibly flushed and dripping with sweat above the nipple line; goosebumps on the arms; skin below the chest looks pale by comparison
the suprapubic catheter bag has drained very little for the past 4 hours
he is in acute distress, sweating, flushed and panicky, sitting in his wheelchair
